description: A long stick used as support for walking or as symbol of authority; to support or guide. 'Tolu' names what extends standing—the third leg that steadies journey.
sound_symbolism: The word stands tall. 'To' plants firm with grounded stability, staff touching earth. Then 'lu' flows upward in support, walker steadied.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'tolu' (the staff) - walking, ceremony, the upright support. One leans and walks by 'roe tolu' (with the staff).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Support without dependence—the staff aids but doesn't carry, assistance that preserves autonomy.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The staff accompanied—pilgrim, shepherd, elder, the walking companion through life's journey.
